title: Fever and Rash - Measles (Rubeola)
chief_complaint: 5-year-old unvaccinated child with high fever 40°C, cough, coryza, conjunctivitis for 4 days, followed by maculopapular rash starting on face and spreading downward. Koplik spots noted on buccal mucosa.

physical_exam.mouth: Koplik spots (white spots on red background) on buccal mucosa opposite molars
physical_exam.skin: Erythematous maculopapular rash, confluent on face and upper trunk, blanching

key_learning_points: 4 Cs: Cough, Coryza, Conjunctivitis + Koplik spots

patient_responses.immunization: She has not received any vaccines
patient_responses.fever: The fever has been very high for 4 days
patient_responses.rash: It started on her face and moved down her body
patient_responses.sick_contacts: Her cousin had similar symptoms 2 weeks ago

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! Classic measles with Koplik spots.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is measles. The 4Cs + Koplik spots + rash pattern are diagnostic.
feedback.attending_note: Measles management: Supportive care, vitamin A (200,000 IU for >1 year), monitor for complications (pneumonia, encephalitis, otitis media). Isolation (airborne) until 4 days after rash onset. Report to public health. This was preventable with vaccination.
